preview_text:
The model forecasts a Brazil victory at 76%, with Haiti holding only a 4% chance to win. Brazil enters as a clear favorite; Haiti's path to points depends substantially on avoiding defeat rather than securing victory. The remaining probability mass concentrates on a draw at 20%.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a moderately competitive match by model standards. A 15-point gap between the top two outcomes indicates Brazil should dominate possession and chances, yet a one-in-five draw probability suggests Haiti can plausibly frustrate play long enough to escape with a point. This is not a mismatch predicted to be a rout.

The sportsbook consensus diverges noticeably from the model. Books assign Brazil a 90% win probability—14 percentage points above the model's 76%—and compress Haiti's chances to 3%. The market views this as substantially more lopsided; the model sees materially higher draw risk. Both sources agree on a Brazil lean, but the consensus is meaningfully more confident in a decisive outcome. No Polymarket data is yet available for cross-reference.
